content: Diagnosis via fasting or non-fasting lipid panel: total cholesterol, HDL-C, triglycerides, calculated or direct LDL-C. Non-HDL cholesterol (total - HDL) increasingly used as it captures all atherogenic particles. Apolipoprotein B (apoB) reflects particle number, may be superior predictor. Lipoprotein(a) measured once in lifetime to identify genetic elevation (>50 mg/dL or >125 nmol/L). Screening: adults 20+ every 4-6 years if low risk; more frequently with risk factors. Children: universal screening once at 9-11 years and again at 17-21; targeted screening earlier if family history. FH diagnosis: Dutch Lipid Clinic Network criteria use LDL level, physical findings (xanthomas, corneal arcus), family history of premature ASCVD/high cholesterol, genetic testing if available. Evaluate secondary causes: TSH, liver enzymes, creatinine, urine protein, glucose/HbA1c, medication review. Risk calculators (ASCVD Risk Estimator, SCORE) guide treatment decisions.
